relationships.description: GenAI Profile Govern actions extend and specialize the AI RMF Govern function with GAI-specific governance requirements including content provenance policies, third-party GAI oversight, and GAI workforce training.
GenAI Profile Map actions extend the AI RMF Map function with GAI-specific context establishment, stakeholder identification, and risk identification for generative AI deployment scenarios.
GenAI Profile Measure actions extend the AI RMF Measure function with GAI-specific metrics for confabulation rates, content provenance tracking, and trustworthiness evaluation of generated outputs.
GenAI Profile Manage actions extend the AI RMF Manage function with GAI-specific risk treatment strategies including content filtering, output monitoring, and incident response for generated content.
Confabulation risk directly relates to the Valid and Reliable trustworthy AI characteristic. GAI systems that confabulate produce invalid outputs, undermining reliability requirements.
GAI data privacy risk relates to the Privacy-Enhanced trustworthy AI characteristic. Training data memorization and prompt leakage create privacy risks beyond traditional AI systems.
GAI harmful bias and homogenization risk relates to the Fair with Harmful Bias Managed characteristic. Generative models can amplify training data biases at scale.
GAI information security risk (prompt injection, model extraction) relates to the Secure and Resilient trustworthy AI characteristic. GAI introduces novel attack surfaces.
Human-AI configuration risk relates to Accountable and Transparent characteristics. Inadequate oversight of GAI systems undermines accountability and transparency.
Information integrity risk from synthetic media and disinformation relates to the Safe characteristic. Large-scale misinformation can endanger public safety and democratic processes.
Confabulation risk should be assessed as part of ISO 42005 impact assessment. Fabricated outputs can have significant downstream impacts on individuals and organizations.
GAI environmental impact risk maps directly to ISO 42005 environmental impact assessment. Both address resource consumption and sustainability concerns of AI systems.
